result:
## Summary

Found the root cause: this site is **mid-pivot** — it used to be "InboxZap" (bulk email cleanup) and is now "Lumina" (AI meeting assistant), but the migration was incomplete. That incompleteness is exactly what was capping organic traffic. Fixed the concrete defects in the repo:

**1. Brand/metadata mismatch (the big one)** — `app/layout.tsx` had `<title>`, meta description, and OG tags all describing InboxZap, while every rendered page is about Lumina. Every Google snippet and social share was advertising the wrong product. Rewrote to match Lumina, and fixed the OG image path (the old one, `/img/og-inboxzap.png`, didn't even exist in `public/`).

**2. Broken core pages** — `/features` and `/pricing` were linked from the nav (and `/pricing` is the homepage's primary "Start free" CTA) but had no corresponding page files — both 404'd. Built real pages for both. I deliberately did **not** invent pricing tiers/dollar figures — the pricing page mirrors only what's already stated ("Start free," contact for team plans).

**3. Dead-end redirect** — `/faq` permanently redirected to `/#faq`, but no `id="faq"` existed on the homepage. Added a real FAQ section there, grounded only in facts already stated elsewhere on the site.

**4. Dead blog** — `app/blog/page.tsx` was a client component fetching `/api/posts`, which doesn't exist, so it always showed an error. GSC shows this blog once had real traffic (`post-1`…`post-12`, up to 3,900 impressions each), but there's no `[slug]` route at all — and that old content is about email cleanup anyway, irrelevant to Lumina now. Converted the blog to a server-rendered index + dynamic `[slug]` route with per-post metadata, and seeded two genuine, on-topic articles (AI meeting assistants, turning notes into action items) as a starting point for a real content engine.

**5. Sitemap** — was listing the (previously 404ing) `/features`/`/pricing` and omitting blog posts entirely. Now includes the real blog posts dynamically.

**What I couldn't do here:** `DISTRIBB_API_KEY` isn't set, so I couldn't pull live keyword volumes, run the GSC-connected audit, or use the backlink exchange — this analysis was done manually from the `gsc/` CSVs and a source-tree audit (confirmed route existence directly from the `app/` file structure, since network access to the dev server wasn't available in this shell). All of the GSC history is old InboxZap queries at terrible positions (30-60), so there's no real "recoverable" traffic there — the priority now is building a real content/keyword strategy for the new "AI meeting assistant" category. Once you've got a Distribb account and GSC connected, running `/gsc-audit` and `/keyword-research` would be the next concrete step, plus adding a real OG image asset.
